rationale: The tally Workflow-path note (ms-panel-tally/SKILL.md lines 16-18, both mirrors byte-identical) is consistent and safe. (1) It accurately describes what /ms-panel returns: cross-checked against plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js Step 5, whose own comment reads 'return verify + tally output verbatim. Never runs the lifecycle merge-terminal command, never consolidates, never authors a consolidated-round file' — this exactly matches the note's claim that verify/tally ran as the workflow's last two steps and their stdout returns verbatim, and that consolidation is NOT done by the workflow (so the skill's Step 2 must still run it). (2) The note explicitly enumerates 'Consolidate, Artifact gates, After a halt - recovery, and Escape hatch' as unchanged on both paths, and these sections (SKILL.md:53-96) are untouched by the diff -- the Allow-branch artifact-gate screen at SKILL.md:30 sits inside Step 1's prose (not exempted-away) and the note only narrows the *mechanical tally-running* part of Step 1, not the judgment screen. This matches AC9 in plan.md verbatim ('Consolidate / Artifact gates / After a halt / Escape hatch survive'). (3) The pre-rendered handoff is coherent with 9cyu.2's shipped workflow -- verified directly against ms-panel.js source, not just the BRIEF's description. No skipped safety step found.
findings: ms-panel-tally/SKILL.md Workflow-path note (plugins + .claude mirrors, byte-identical per diff -q) matches ms-panel.js Step 5 comment verbatim in substance: workflow runs `mindspec panel verify` then `mindspec panel tally` and returns stdout unmodified, never consolidates, never invokes mindspec complete.

The Artifact-gate Allow-screen (SKILL.md:30, 'Before handing off to the merge terminal on an Allow, screen...') remains textually inside Step 1 and is not gated behind a skills-path-only callout, so it still applies when Step 1 is 'just reading' the workflow's pre-rendered tally output.

Consolidate (Step 2), Artifact gates, After a halt - recovery, and Escape hatch sections are byte-for-byte unchanged by the diff (+4 lines only, all in the new note) and are the exact four sections plan.md AC9 requires to survive.
runbook.md doc-sync entry (.mindspec/domains/workflow/runbook.md) accurately restates the note's content, including the explicit 'retained unchanged on both paths' line for the same five sections.

No mismatch found between the note's description and ms-bead-cycle/SKILL.md's still-skills-path-only sequence diagram, but that's out of scope for Bead 3 (Bead 3 only touches ms-panel-run/ms-panel-tally/runbook per plan.md) and is R6's lens, not a Bead-3 defect.
